title.value: A SURVEY OF AUTOMATIC GENERATION OF SOURCE CODE COMMENTS: ALGORITHMS AND TECHNIQUES
title.evidence: A SURVEY OF AUTOMATIC GENERATION OF SOURCE CODE COMMENTS: ALGORITHMS AND TECHNIQUES

authors.evidence:
Xiaotao Song School of Software Taiyuan University of Technology, China songxiaotao@tyut.edu.cn
Hailong Sun∗, Xu Wang and Jiafei Yan SKLSDE Lab, School of Computer Science and Engineering Beijing Advanced Innovation Center for Big Data and Brain Computing Beihang University, Beijing, China sunhl@buaa.edu.cn

abstract.value: This paper surveys automatic code commenting research, analyzing challenges, classifying algorithms, and assessing comment quality. It aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the state-of-the-art in automatic code comment generation, discussing future research directions.
abstract.evidence: As an integral part of source code files, code comments help improve program readability and comprehension. However, developers sometimes do not comment on their program code adequately due to the incurred extra efforts, lack of relevant knowledge, unawareness of the importance of code commenting or some other factors. To solve these problems of code comments, researchers have been concerned with generating code comments automatically. In this work, we aim at conducting a survey of automatic code commenting researches.

problem_description.value: The problem addressed is the inadequacy, absence, or mismatch of code comments with source code, which affects software understanding, reusability, and maintenance.
problem_description.evidence: As a result, code comments can be inadequate, absent or even mismatched with source code, which affects the understanding, reusing and the maintenance of software.

value: Building effective source code models to capture structural and semantic features.
Designing natural language text generation methods from structured code.
Establishing objective quality assessment criteria for generated comments.
challenges.evidence: However, generating satisfying code comments remains a challenging issue. The fundamental reason lies in the fact that programming languages are different from natural languages in nature.

data_preparation.value: Data preparation involves collecting datasets containing source code and corresponding comments from open source communities or websites to train and validate commenting models.
data_preparation.evidence: First, data collection to construct datasets for comment generation systems. These data are used for training, validating and testing models, extracting code and the corresponding comments.

provenance.section_header: Challenges of Automatic Code Commenting and Research Framework

value: Other automatic code comment generation algorithms
algorithm_classification.evidence: Existing algorithms mainly fall into three categories: information retrieval based algorithms, deep neural networks based algorithms and other automatic code comment generation algorithms.

evaluation_metrics.evidence: The studies on comment quality assessment and reference literature are shown in Figure 5. Khamis et al. [20] present JavadocMiner, a tool that can analyze the quality of Javadoc comments automatically.

provenance.section_header: Quality Evaluation Criteria of Code Comments

evaluation_metrics.notes: Common metrics for evaluating comment quality.

experiment_design.value: The experiments involve systematic literature review and analysis of 32 representative papers from 2010 to 2018, focusing on the algorithms and trends in automatic comment generation.
experiment_design.evidence: To provide a comprehensive survey of the emerging trends in code comment generation automatically, we systematically reviewed the literature from 2010 to 2018 and select 32 representative papers from 59 papers that were published in the last ten years.

provenance.section_header: Trends of the development of code commenting techniques

value: Increased research interest in deep neural network techniques for code comment generation.
Diverse algorithms with varied effectiveness based on different datasets.
results.evidence: The figure indicates a relevant increase of interest and results: almost half of the papers have been published in 2015-2018, and more than 60% of papers appeared after 2014.

provenance.section_header: Trends of the development of code commenting techniques

findings.value: The survey identifies key challenges in automatic code commenting, including the need for effective source code models, text generation methods, and quality assessment criteria.
findings.evidence: By summarizing and analyzing three main classes of automatic code comment generation techniques, we present the future research opportunities.

comparative_analysis.value: The paper compares existing algorithms based on their effectiveness in generating comments, highlighting the transition from information retrieval methods to deep learning approaches.
comparative_analysis.evidence: With the development of artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies, researchers continually apply the emerging techniques to automatic comment generation researches, such as deep learning algorithm.

provenance.section_header: Algorithms of Automatic Generation of Code Comments

comparative_analysis.notes: Highlights the evolution of methods in the field.

datasets_used.evidence: The datasets for validation in existing studies usually varies across commenting systems. These datasets come from three sources: open source projects from GitHub software repositories, Q&A sites in Stack Overflow, and communication and discussion information, like emails, among developers.
datasets_used.notes: Critical for replicating the study's results.

future_directions.value: Future research should focus on integrating deep learning with other models, unifying datasets for comment generation, and enhancing quality assessment metrics.
future_directions.evidence: The combination of multiple models for representing source code is well suitable for the solution of code comment generation problems.
